19	// LLMConfig parameterizes an OpenAI-compatible backend (copilot-proxy, Ollama,
20	// OpenRouter, LiteLLM, or a generic base_url+key endpoint).
21	type LLMConfig struct {
22		Name      string        // "copilot", "ollama", "openrouter", ...
23		BaseURL   string        // e.g. "http://localhost:4141/v1"
24		Model     string        // e.g. "claude-sonnet-5"
25		APIKeyEnv string        // env var holding the key; "" => no Authorization header
26		Timeout   time.Duration // per-request timeout (0 => 60s)
27	}
28	
29	// LLMEngine talks to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint.
30	type LLMEngine struct {
31		cfg  LLMConfig
32		key  string
33		http *http.Client
34	}
35	
36	// NewLLM builds an LLM engine, resolving the API key once from the environment.
37	func NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {
38		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
39			cfg.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
40		}
41		key := ""
42		if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
43			key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
44		}
45		return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
46	}
47	
48	// Name returns the provider name (e.g. "copilot").
49	func (e *LLMEngine) Name() string { return e.cfg.Name }
50	
51	// Supports reports that LLM engines translate. (Dictionary lookups route to the
52	// dedicated dictionary engine in v1, so the chain never sends dict mode here.)
53	func (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
54	
55	// auth adds the bearer token only when a key is configured. copilot-proxy needs
56	// no Authorization header, so an empty APIKeyEnv yields no header at all.
57	func (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {
58		if e.key != "" {
59			r.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+e.key)
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// endpoint joins the base URL with a path segment.
64	func (e *LLMEngine) endpoint(path string) string {
65		return strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.BaseURL, "/") + path
66	}
67	
68	// Available is a cheap health probe: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short timeout.
69	func (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
70		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond)
71		defer cancel()
72		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
73		if err != nil {
74			return false
75		}
76		e.auth(req)
77		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
78		if err != nil {
79			return false
80		}
81		defer resp.Body.Close()
82		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
83		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
84	}
85

88	func (e *LLMEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
89		return "", nil
90	}

95		Role    string `json:"role"`
96		Content string `json:"content"`
97	}
98

103	}

111		} `json:"error"`
112	}

114	// streamDelta is one OpenAI SSE chunk (stream:true).
115	type streamDelta struct {
116		Choices []struct {
117			Delta struct {
118				Content string `json:"content"`
119			} `json:"delta"`
120		} `json:"choices"`
121	}

125	const anthropicMaxTokens = 4096
126	
127	type anthropicRequest struct {
128		Model     string             `json:"model"`
129		MaxTokens int                `json:"max_tokens"`
130		System    string             `json:"system,omitempty"`
131		Messages  []anthropicMessage `json:"messages"`
132		Stream    bool               `json:"stream"`
133	}

135	type anthropicMessage struct {
136		Role    string `json:"role"`
137		Content string `json:"content"`
138	}
139	
140	type anthropicResponse struct {
141		Content []struct {
142			Type string `json:"type"`
143			Text string `json:"text"`
144		} `json:"content"`
145		Error *struct {
146			Message string `json:"message"`
147		} `json:"error"`
148	}

150	type anthropicStreamEvent struct {
151		Type  string `json:"type"`
152		Delta struct {
153			Type string `json:"type"`
154			Text string `json:"text"`
155		} `json:"delta"`
156		Error *struct {
157			Message string `json:"message"`
158		} `json:"error"`
159	}
160	
161	// isAnthropicModel reports whether a model id must use the Anthropic Messages API
162	// (/v1/messages) rather than /chat/completions. copilot-proxy serves Claude
163	// models ONLY via the messages endpoint — they return HTTP 400
164	// "model_not_supported" on /chat/completions despite being listed in /v1/models.
165	func isAnthropicModel(model string) bool {
166		return strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(model), "claude")
167	}
168	
169	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
170	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
171	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
172	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
173		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
174			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
175		}
176		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
177		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
178		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
179		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
180			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
181		}
182		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
183			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
184		}
185		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
186	}
187	
188	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
189	type ModelLister interface {
190		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
191	}
192	
193	type modelsResponse struct {
194		Data []struct {
195			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
196			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
197		} `json:"data"`
198	}
199	
200	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
201	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
202	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
203	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
204		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
205		defer cancel()
206		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
207		if err != nil {
208			return nil, err
209		}
210		e.auth(req)
211		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
212		if err != nil {
213			return nil, err
214		}
215		defer resp.Body.Close()
216		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
217			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
218		}
219		var mr modelsResponse
220		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
221			return nil, err
222		}
223		var out []string
224		for _, m := range mr.Data {
225			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
226				out = append(out, m.ID)
227			}
228		}
229		sort.Strings(out)
230		return out, nil
231	}
232	
233	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
234	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
235		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
236			return true // routed via /v1/messages
237		}
238		for _, ep := range endpoints {
239			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
240				return true
241			}
242		}
243		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
244		return len(endpoints) == 0
245	}
246	
247	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
248	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
249		res := &TranslateResult{
250			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
251			Target:      req.Target,
252			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
253			Model:       model,
254		}
255		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
256		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
257		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
258			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
259		}
260		return res
261	}
262	
263	// translateOpenAI uses the OpenAI /chat/completions endpoint.
264	func (e *LLMEngine) translateOpenAI(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
265		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
266		body := chatRequest{
267			Model: model,
268			Messages: []chatMessage{
269				{Role: "system", Content: system},
270				{Role: "user", Content: user},
271			},
272			Stream: req.Stream,
273		}
274		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
275		if err != nil {
276			return nil, err
277		}
278		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/chat/completions"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
279		if err != nil {
280			return nil, err
281		}
282		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
283		if req.Stream {
284			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
285		}
286		e.auth(httpReq)
287	
288		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffered so a fast stream doesn't block on renders
289		go func() {
290			defer close(ch)
291			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
292			if err != nil {
293				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
294				return
295			}
296			defer resp.Body.Close()
297			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
298				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
299				return
300			}
301	
302			var full strings.Builder
303			if req.Stream {
304				if err := e.readSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full); err != nil {
305					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
306					return
307				}
308			} else {
309				var cr chatResponse
310				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {
311					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
312					return
313				}
314				if cr.Error != nil {
315					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, cr.Error.Message)}
316					return
317				}
318				if len(cr.Choices) == 0 {
319					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: empty response", e.cfg.Name)}
320					return
321				}
322				full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)
323			}
324			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)}
325		}()
326		return ch, nil
327	}
328	
329	// translateAnthropic uses the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).
330	func (e *LLMEngine) translateAnthropic(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
331		system, user := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
332		body := anthropicRequest{
333			Model:     model,
334			MaxTokens: anthropicMaxTokens,
335			System:    system,
336			Messages:  []anthropicMessage{{Role: "user", Content: user}},
337			Stream:    req.Stream,
338		}
339		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
340		if err != nil {
341			return nil, err
342		}
343		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/messages"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
344		if err != nil {
345			return nil, err
346		}
347		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
348		httpReq.Header.Set("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
349		if req.Stream {
350			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
351		}
352		e.anthropicAuth(httpReq)
353	
354		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32)
355		go func() {
356			defer close(ch)
357			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
358			if err != nil {
359				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
360				return
361			}
362			defer resp.Body.Close()
363			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
364				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
365				return
366			}
367	
368			var full strings.Builder
369			if req.Stream {
370				if err := e.readAnthropicSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full); err != nil {
371					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
372					return
373				}
374			} else {
375				var ar anthropicResponse
376				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&ar); err != nil {
377					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
378					return
379				}
380				if ar.Error != nil {
381					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ar.Error.Message)}
382					return
383				}
384				for _, c := range ar.Content {
385					if c.Type == "text" {
386						full.WriteString(c.Text)
387					}
388				}
389			}
390			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: e.finalize(full.String(), model, req)}
391		}()
392		return ch, nil
393	}
394	
395	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
396	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
397	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
398		if e.key != "" {
399			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
400		}
401	}
402	
403	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
404	// delta and accumulating the full text into full.
405	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) error {
406		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
407		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
408		for sc.Scan() {
409			line := sc.Text()
410			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
411				continue
412			}
413			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
414			if payload == "[DONE]" {
415				break
416			}
417			var d streamDelta
418			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
419				continue
420			}
421			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
422			if tok == "" {
423				continue
424			}
425			full.WriteString(tok)
426			select {
427			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
428			case <-ctx.Done():
429				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
430			}
431		}
432		return sc.Err()
433	}
434	
435	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
436	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full.
437	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) error {
438		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
439		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
440		for sc.Scan() {
441			line := sc.Text()
442			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
443				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
444			}
445			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
446			if payload == "" {
447				continue
448			}
449			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
450			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
451				continue
452			}
453			if ev.Error != nil {
454				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
455			}
456			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
457				continue
458			}
459			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
460			select {
461			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
462			case <-ctx.Done():
463				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
464			}
465		}
466		return sc.Err()
467	}
468	
469	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
470	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
471		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
472		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
473		if msg == "" {
474			msg = resp.Status
475		}
476		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
477	}
478
```

76	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
77	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
78	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
79	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
80		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
81			return m, nil
82		}
83		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
84		case engine.ChunkToken:
85			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
86			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
87			m.vp.GotoBottom()

89	
90		case engine.ChunkDone:

95			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
96				m.result = msg.chunk.Result

101				if msg.chunk.Result.Model != "" {

106				// Don't persist a suggestions-only result (empty translation, no entry).

108					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
109				}

111			}
112			return m, nil
113

115			m.inflight = 0
116			m.status = statusError
117			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
118			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))

256	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
257	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
258	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
259	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
260	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
261		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
262		if text == "" {
263			m.status = statusIdle
264			return m, nil
265		}
266		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
267		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
268			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
269		}
270		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
271		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
272		if !force {
273			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
274				m.cancelInflight()
275				m.status = statusDone
276				m.err = nil
277				m.result = cached
278				if cached.Engine != "" {
279					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
280				}
281				if cached.Model != "" {
282					m.curModel = cached.Model
283				}
284				m.streamBuf = ""
285				m.lastInput = text
286				m.lastDoneKey = key
287				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
288				return m, nil
289			}
290		}
291		m.seq++
292		if m.cancel != nil {
293			m.cancel()
294		}
295		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
296		m.cancel = cancel
297		m.inflight = m.seq
298		m.status = statusTranslating
299		m.streamBuf = ""
300		m.err = nil
301		m.result = nil
302		m.lastInput = text
303		m.pendingKey = key
304		m.vp.SetContent(m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render("translating…")) // fixed-height placeholder
305	
306		seq := m.seq
307		ne := m.active()
308		target := m.target
309		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate {
310			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
311		}
312		req := engine.Request{
313			Text:          text,
314			Source:        m.source,
315			Target:        target,
316			Mode:          ne.Mode,
317			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
318			Model:         m.modelOverride,
319			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
320			Preset:        m.preset,
321			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
322		}
323		ch, err := ne.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
324		if err != nil {
325			e := err
326			return m, func() tea.Msg {
327				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
328			}
329		}
330		m.stream = ch
331		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
332	}

482	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
483	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
484		src := m.source
485		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
486			src = res.DetectedSource
487		}
488		target := m.target
489		if res.Target != "" {
490			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
491		}
492		return store.Record{
493			SourceLang:   src,
494			TargetLang:   target,
495			Engine:       res.Engine,
496			Model:        res.Model,
497			Input:        m.lastInput,
498			Output:       res.Translation,
499			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
500			Notes:        res.Notes,
501		}
502	}
503	
504	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. Widths account for
505	// each box's border (2) and horizontal padding (2).
506	func (m *Model) relayout() {
507		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
508			return
509		}
510		const inputH = 4
511		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
512		compW := m.width - 4
513		if compW < 1 {
514			compW = 1
515		}
516		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
517		if resultH < 3 {
518			resultH = 3
519		}
520		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
521		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
522		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
523		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
524		m.resultH = resultH
525		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
526		if overlayH < 1 {
527			overlayH = 1
528		}
529		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
530		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
531		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
532		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
533		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
534	}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm tracing through the stream consumption logic—each token gets appended to the buffer via `waitStream`, and when `ChunkDone` arrives, it finalizes and renders the result. The display truncation seems to be happening somewhere in this flow, but I need to figure out exactly where the cutoff occurs.

Looking at the screenshot output with "detected: english" visible, that suggests the final result was actually rendered, which means `ChunkDone` fired. But if the full text was complete at that point, the truncation must be in the accumulated stream itself—the SSE stream from the server ended prematurely before the model finished generating. The `readAnthropicSSE` loop just exits cleanly when the stream closes, so there's no error signal, just a partial result. This could be caused by the HTTP client timeout, which covers the entire request duration including the response body read. me reconsider the timeout and stop reason handling. The HTTP client timeout is a hard deadline on the entire request including the streaming body, set to 60 seconds, which should be plenty for fast translation. But I'm realizing the code might not be properly detecting the `stop_reason` field in the message_delta events — it only looks at content_block_delta for text chunks, so if the model actually hit max_tokens or another stop condition, that signal would be missed and the stream would appear to end prematurely.

The mid-word truncation ("英文翻" without "譯") is the real clue here — that's not how models naturally stop. They'd finish at a sentence boundary. This points to either a client-side timeout cutting off the connection or the server/proxy dropping the stream unexpectedly.

I'm checking the error handling in the goroutine that reads the SSE stream. The code sends a ChunkError if readAnthropicSSE returns an error, but if it returns nil on a clean EOF, it sends ChunkDone with whatever partial content accumulated. So the question is whether the scanner is actually hitting an error that's being silently swallowed, or if it's genuinely seeing EOF without an error.

The most likely culprit is that copilot-proxy or the upstream GitHub Copilot API is terminating the connection early, which would cause the stream to end cleanly without an error. There's also the possibility of an HTTP timeout, which I should reconsider. I think the real issue might be that the copilot-proxy is treating an incomplete stream as a successful completion, and separately the upstream stream from GitHub Copilot's backend is hitting its own token limit or max response size before the full generation finishes.

Let me focus on what I can actually verify: whether the stream handling properly detects the `stop_reason` field when the upstream returns `max_tokens`, and whether copilot-proxy might be using a lower default `max_tokens` than expected or ignoring the 4096 limit we're setting.

I should check the project's documentation and history — the TODO, backlog, and pitfalls sections might have notes about truncation issues with copilot-proxy.

10	The local **copilot-proxy** (`http://localhost:4141`, no auth) exposes multiple endpoints and each model only works on specific ones. A model listed in `GET /v1/models` is NOT necessarily usable on `/chat/completions`.
11	
12	Key gotcha (cost hours): **Claude models are served only via the Anthropic Messages API `POST /v1/messages`**, not `/chat/completions` (they return `HTTP 400 model_not_supported` there, despite listing `/chat/completions` in their `supported_endpoints`). Use the Anthropic request shape: `{"model","max_tokens","system","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"…"}],"stream":true}`, header `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`, no key needed. Streaming is Anthropic SSE (`content_block_delta` → `delta.text`).
13	
14	- **Work on `/chat/completions`** (OpenAI style): `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5-mini`, `gemini-3.5-flash`, `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, and `claude-sonnet-5` (Claude also works there, but haiku/opus do not — route ALL Claude via /v1/messages for consistency).
15	- **Work on `/v1/messages`** (Anthropic style): all `claude-*` — verified `claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-sonnet-5`, `claude-opus-4-8`.
16	- **Need `/responses`** (OpenAI Responses API, NOT chat): `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-5.4-mini` — error "not accessible via the /chat/completions endpoint".
17	
18	To pick an endpoint, read each model's `supported_endpoints` in `/v1/models` (e.g. gpt-5.5 lists only `['/responses','ws:/responses']`). Use hyphenated ids, strip any `[1m]` suffix, never dated ids. In a fallback chain a rejected model silently downgrades to the next engine (e.g. Ollama `llama3.2:3b`) — looks like "the LLM got dumber"; check the model/endpoint first. The `translate` CLI in this repo routes `claude-*` to /v1/messages automatically (fast=haiku, default=sonnet-5, max=opus-4-8).

The real issue is in how `readAnthropicSSE` handles the stream — it treats a clean EOF as success even when the stream never reached the `message_stop` event that signals completion. The code just accumulates whatever text deltas arrived and returns without error, so incomplete responses slip through silently. If the upstream connection dropped early or the proxy terminated the stream prematurely, we'd see exactly this kind of mid-word cutoff. The fix would be to verify we actually received `message_stop` before considering the stream complete, otherwise return an error instead of partial content.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm identifying the root cause now. The issue is that the Anthropic SSE reader treats any clean end-of-stream as a successful completion, only collecting the text delta events and returning nothing when it hits EOF, which causes the translation to cut off mid-word.

The real problem is that the code never validates whether the terminal `message_stop` event actually arrived or checks the `stop_reason` field to distinguish between a natural end versus hitting `max_tokens`. When copilot-proxy drops the connection mid-generation, the partial output gets silently treated as finished. The mid-word cutoff at 「英文翻」 is the telltale sign — language models don't naturally stop mid-word, they stop at sentence boundaries. This points to a dropped stream, not a model decision. The 60-second timeout on the HTTP client could also truncate long streams the same way, though that's less likely here given the output size.

I should verify the OpenAI path has the same vulnerability — it does, since `readSSE` treats EOF as success and doesn't parse `finish_reason` either. The fix is straightforward: detect when `message_stop` is missing from the Anthropic stream or when `finish_reason` is absent from OpenAI, and flag those as incomplete rather than silently accepting partial output.

One thing to confirm: copilot-proxy faithfully re-emits Anthropic SSE including `message_delta` and `message_stop`, so checking for those events should reliably catch truncation. I'll present the diagnosis and a defensive fix, then offer to implement it. Since the user wrote in Traditional Chinese, I'll respond in that language.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 06:32:37Z)**_

確認了。這不是模型「只翻一半就自然停下」——語言模型不會停在詞中間(「英文翻」缺了「譯」,正常結束會落在標點或句尾)。這是**串流被中途截斷,但程式把它當成正常完成**。

## 問題根因

在 `internal/engine/llm.go` 的 `readAnthropicSSE`(claude-sonnet-5 走 `/v1/messages` 這條路):

```go
func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(...) error {
    for sc.Scan() {
        // 只處理 content_block_delta / text_delta,累加文字
        ...
    }
    return sc.Err()   // ← EOF 時回傳 nil
}
```

然後 `translateAnthropic` 的邏輯是:

```go
if err := e.readAnthropicSSE(...); err != nil {
    ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
    return
}
ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: e.finalize(full.String(), ...)}  // ← 只要沒 error 就當成功
```

關鍵缺口:**只要串流以「乾淨的 EOF」結束,不管內容完不完整,都送 `ChunkDone` 當作翻譯完成。** 它完全沒有檢查:

1. 是否收到終結事件 `message_stop`(Anthropic 串流協定保證的收尾);
2. `message_delta` 裡的 `stop_reason`(`end_turn` 正常完成 vs `max_tokens` 被截)。

所以當 **copilot-proxy / Copilot 上游把 SSE 連線中途斷掉**(這是間歇性行為),已到達的半截文字就被靜默地當成完整結果 render 出來,還配上 `detected: english`,看起來一切正常。

排除的其他可能:
- **不是 `max_tokens`**:上限是 4096,這段輸出約 70 字 ≈ 150 tokens,遠遠不到。
- **不是 prompt 叫它停**:concise prompt 只要求「輸出翻譯文字」,沒有 stop sequence。
- **60s 全域 timeout**(`http.Client{Timeout}` 涵蓋整個串流讀取)是同一類潛在 bug,但這次輸出很短、很快,不太可能是它——不過它會走同一條「靜默半成品」路徑。

OpenAI 那條 `readSSE` 也有一樣的毛病:沒收到 `[DONE]` 就 EOF、或 `finish_reason` 沒解析,同樣會靜默截斷。

## 建議修法

讓截斷「看得見」,而不是偽裝成成功:

1. `readAnthropicSSE` 追蹤是否看到 `message_stop`(以及 `message_delta.stop_reason`);串流結束時若沒收到收尾事件 → 回傳 error(或在結果上標記 `truncated`),讓 `handleStream` 走 `ChunkError` 顯示「✗ 串流中斷,請按 Enter 重試」。
2. 同樣在 OpenAI 的 `readSSE` 檢查 `[DONE]` / `finish_reason`。
3. (可選)把 `stop_reason == "max_tokens"` 也顯式標出,並考慮把串流用的 `http.Client.Timeout` 拿掉、改由 `ctx` 控制 deadline,避免長輸出被 60s 砍掉還當成功。

要我直接動手改嗎?我建議先做第 1、2 點(核心 bug),把靜默截斷變成明確的錯誤 + 可重試。這也值得在 `pitfalls/` 記一筆。

## 1. Type definitions (internal/engine/engine.go)

All the requested types live in **`engine.go`** (not spread across files). `errors.go` holds only sentinels.

**`Request`** — engine.go:23-36

type Request struct {
	Text    string
	Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
	Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
	Mode    Mode
	MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
	Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
	Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
	ModelProvider string
	Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
	Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
}
```

**`TranslateResult`** — engine.go:40-62 (all fields)
```go
type TranslateResult struct {
	Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
	DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
	Target         string   `json:"target"`
	Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
	Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
	Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
	Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
	Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
	Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
	Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`

}
```
Note the `Warnings []string` field — engine.go:49-50 describes it as recording "non-fatal problems ... so a downgrade is never silent."

**`ChunkKind` + constants** — engine.go:87-96
```go
type ChunkKind int

const (
	// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
	ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
	// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
	ChunkDone
	// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
	ChunkError
)
```

**`Chunk`** — engine.go:99-104
```go
type Chunk struct {
	Kind   ChunkKind
	Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
	Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
	Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
}
```

**Sentinel errors (errors.go, whole file)** — style is `var (...)` block, each `errors.New("engine: <message>")` lowercase, package-prefixed, with a doc comment per var:
```go
var (
	ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
	ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
	ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
	ErrAllEnginesFailed = errors.New("engine: all engines failed")
	ErrNoDictEntry = errors.New("engine: no dictionary entry")

```
`ErrNoResult` ("engine: stream closed without a result") is conceptually adjacent to the truncation bug — it is returned by `Drain` (engine.go:152) when a channel closes with no terminal chunk at all.

## 2. Existing tests — NONE EXIST

There are **zero `*_test.go` files anywhere in the repository** (confirmed via `find ... -name "*_test.go"` returning nothing, and grep for `httptest`/`readSSE`/`Translate` in test files returning nothing). There is no `httptest` fake-SSE-server pattern to match. A truncation test would be the first test in `internal/engine/`. The engine is driven at runtime by the `Drain` helper (engine.go:139-153) and the TUI Update loop, and `single()` (engine.go:125-134) is the buffered-channel helper for non-streaming engines — those are the closest existing "shape" references for constructing Chunk channels in a test.

## 3. Pitfalls house style (pitfalls/README.md + go-install-module-path-mismatch.md)

**Frontmatter** is not YAML — it is bold-label lines immediately under the H1. Required fields (README.md:57-63, matched exactly by the sample file):
```markdown
# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>

**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
**First seen**: YYYY-MM
**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
```

**Section structure** (README.md:65-84) — H2 sections in this order:
- `## Symptom` — full verbatim error messages (preserves grep-ability)
- `## Root cause` — why, with source/docs/upstream link
- `## Workaround` — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
- `## Prevention` — how to avoid it again
- `## Related` — links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues

The real file `go-install-module-path-mismatch.md` follows this exactly: H1 symptom-title, the four bold labels (its `**Status**: fixed — module renamed to the GitHub path.`), then `## Symptom` / `## Root cause` / `## Workaround` / `## Prevention` / `## Related`. Slugs are kebab-case describing the symptom.

**Indexing** — YES, there is a manual index table in README.md:87-94 that a new pitfall must be added to, kept alphabetical:
```markdown
## Index

Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.

| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `duplicate-translate-on-path-...` | ... | workaround; fix in TODO P2 |
| `go-install-module-path-mismatch` | `module declares its path as: translate`, ... | fixed (module renamed) |
| `gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir` | ... | workaround (pin GOBIN) |
```
There is also a separate "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table (README.md:96-104) for traps documented elsewhere — not relevant for a net-new pitfall. The per-doc template is external, in the `project-knowledge-harness` skill (`pitfall-doc.md.template`), referenced at README.md:50-52. Related `TODO.md` links appear in pitfall `## Related` sections (e.g. the sample's line 63 references a `TODO.md` "Done" entry) — so if you add a TODO item for the fix, cross-linking it is the house convention.

## 4. stop_reason / finish_reason / message_stop / [DONE] handling

Only **one** of these four appears anywhere in `internal/engine/`:

- **`[DONE]`** — handled, OpenAI path only, llm.go:414-416:
  ```go
  if payload == "[DONE]" {
  	break
  }
  ```
  This is the OpenAI-style terminal sentinel. Note it `break`s the same as a natural loop exit — there is no flag recording that `[DONE]` was actually seen, so a stream that ends without `[DONE]` (truncation) is indistinguishable from a clean finish here.

- **`stop_reason`, `finish_reason`, `message_stop`** — **not present anywhere** in `internal/engine/` (grep returned no matches). The wire structs confirm they are never parsed:
  - `streamDelta` (llm.go:114-121) parses only `choices[].delta.content` — no `finish_reason`.
  - `anthropicStreamEvent` (llm.go:150-159) parses only `type`, `delta.{type,text}`, and `error.message` — no `message_stop`/`stop_reason` type handling. The Anthropic reader (llm.go:456) explicitly skips every event whose `Type != "content_block_delta"`, so `message_stop` / `message_delta` (which carries `stop_reason`) events are silently ignored.

**Both readers return `sc.Err()` at the end** (readSSE llm.go:432, readAnthropicSSE llm.go:466). A clean EOF makes `bufio.Scanner` return `nil` from `Err()`, so both readers return `nil` on a truncated-but-cleanly-closed stream, and the caller (llm.go:369-373 / 390) then emits a normal `ChunkDone` with whatever partial text accumulated in `full`. This is exactly the silent-truncation path described in the bug: neither the OpenAI nor the Anthropic reader records having seen its terminal marker (`[DONE]` / `message_stop`), so completeness is never asserted.

Relevant call sites for context: the streaming dispatch that turns a nil reader-return into `ChunkDone` is at llm.go:368-390 (Anthropic branch shown; the OpenAI `translate`/`readSSE` caller has the analogous structure earlier in the file).
</output>
```

1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	}
37	
38	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
39	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
40	type TranslateResult struct {
41		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
42		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
43		Target         string   `json:"target"`
44		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
45		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
46		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
47	
48		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
49		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
50		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
51	
52		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
53		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
54		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
55	
56		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
57		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
58		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
59		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
60		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
61		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
62	}
63	
64	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
65	type DictEntry struct {
66		Word      string    `json:"word"`
67		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
68		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
69		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
70	}
71	
72	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
73	type Meaning struct {
74		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
75		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
76		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
77		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
78	}
79	
80	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
81	type Definition struct {
82		Text    string `json:"definition"`
83		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
84	}
85	
86	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
87	type ChunkKind int
88	
89	const (
90		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
91		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
92		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
93		ChunkDone
94		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
95		ChunkError
96	)
97	
98	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
99	type Chunk struct {
100		Kind   ChunkKind
101		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
102		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
103		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
104	}
105	
106	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
107	//
108	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
109	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
110	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
111	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
112	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
113	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
114	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
115	type Engine interface {
116		Name() string
117		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
118		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
119		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
120		Supports(m Mode) bool
121	}
122	
123	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
124	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
125	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
126		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
127		if err != nil {
128			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
129		} else {
130			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
131		}
132		close(ch)
133		return ch
134	}
135	
136	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
137	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
138	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
139	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
140		for c := range ch {
141			switch c.Kind {
142			case ChunkToken:
143				if onToken != nil {
144					onToken(c.Text)
145				}
146			case ChunkDone:
147				return c.Result, nil
148			case ChunkError:
149				return nil, c.Err
150			}
151		}
152		return nil, ErrNoResult
153	}
154
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is maintainer-facing repo metadata. It lives in the git repo but
9	never reaches users — `go install` / `go build` compile only Go sources, so
10	`TODO.md`, `backlog/`, and `pitfalls/` have no effect on the built binary.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/go-install-module-path-mismatch.md`

```text
1	# go install fails: "module declares its path as: translate" but required as github.com/...
2	
3	**Symptoms** (grep this section): `go install` error `module declares its
4	path as: translate`, `but was required as: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`,
5	`parsing go.mod`; `go install github.com/...@latest` refuses to install a repo
6	whose `go.mod` says `module translate`.
7	**First seen**: 2026-07
8	**Affects**: any Go module whose `module` path in `go.mod` doesn't match its
9	import/repo URL; `go install <path>@<ver>`; Go 1.16+ (module-aware install).
10	**Status**: fixed — module renamed to the GitHub path.
11	
12	## Symptom
13	
14	```
15	$ go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest
16	go: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@v0.1.0: parsing go.mod:
17		module declares its path as: translate
18		        but was required as: github.com/daviddwlee84/translate
19	```
20	
21	The project built fine locally (`go build .`) with `module translate` in
22	`go.mod` and imports like `"translate/internal/config"`. It only breaks when you
23	try to `go install` it by its resolvable URL, because the declared module path
24	and the requested path disagree.
25	
26	## Root cause
27	
28	`go install <pkg>@<version>` fetches the module and requires that the path in the
29	downloaded `go.mod` **exactly equals** the path you asked for. A local-only module
30	name (`module translate`) is legal for `go build` in the working tree but is not a
31	resolvable import path — `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`
32	downloads the repo, reads `module translate`, and rejects the mismatch. This is Go
33	enforcing that a module's identity is its import path.
34	
35	## Workaround
36	
37	Rename the module to the repo URL and rewrite every internal import prefix in the
38	same pass, then rebuild:
39	
40	```sh
41	go mod edit -module github.com/daviddwlee84/translate
42	# rewrite internal imports "translate/..." -> "github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/..."
43	grep -rl '"translate/' --include='*.go' . \
44	  | xargs sed -i '' 's#"translate/#"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/#g'   # macOS BSD sed: -i ''
45	go build ./... && go vet ./...
46	```
47	
48	Guard: only string literals `"translate/…"` are import paths here; bare
49	`"translate"` occurrences were non-import string literals (a config subdir const
50	and a keybinding help string), so the prefixed replace was safe. Verify with
51	`grep -rn '"translate/' cmd internal main.go` returning nothing.
52	
53	## Prevention
54	
55	- When creating a Go project you might ever `go install`/publish, name the module
56	  after its future repo URL from day one: `go mod init github.com/<you>/<repo>`.
57	- Binary name from `go install` is the **last path element** of the main package's
58	  module path — keeping the repo named `translate` keeps the binary `translate`.
59	
60	## Related
61	
62	- Sibling: [gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md](gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir.md)
63	- Shipped fix: `TODO.md` Done "Publish as a public repo + `go install`"
64	- Go docs: https://go.dev/ref/mod#go-install
65
```
</details></tool-use>

395	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
396	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
397	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
398		if e.key != "" {
399			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
400		}
401	}
402	
403	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
404	// delta and accumulating the full text into full.
405	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) error {
406		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
407		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
408		for sc.Scan() {
409			line := sc.Text()
410			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
411				continue
412			}
413			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
414			if payload == "[DONE]" {
415				break
416			}
417			var d streamDelta
418			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
419				continue
420			}
421			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
422			if tok == "" {
423				continue
424			}
425			full.WriteString(tok)
426			select {
427			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
428			case <-ctx.Done():
429				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
430			}
431		}
432		return sc.Err()
433	}
434	
435	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
436	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full.
437	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) error {
438		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
439		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
440		for sc.Scan() {
441			line := sc.Text()
442			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
443				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
444			}
445			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
446			if payload == "" {
447				continue
448			}
449			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
450			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
451				continue
452			}
453			if ev.Error != nil {
454				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
455			}
456			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
457				continue
458			}
459			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
460			select {
461			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
462			case <-ctx.Done():
463				return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
464			}
465		}
466		return sc.Err()
467	}
468	
469	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
470	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
471		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
472		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
473		if msg == "" {
474			msg = resp.Status
475		}
476		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
477	}
478

I have all the facts. Here is the complete report.

## 1. Type definitions (internal/engine/engine.go)

All the requested types live in **`engine.go`** (not spread across files). `errors.go` holds only sentinels.

**`Request`** — engine.go:23-36
```go
type Request struct {
	Text    string
	Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
	Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
	Mode    Mode
	MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
	Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
	Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
	ModelProvider string
	Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
	Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
}
```

**`TranslateResult`** — engine.go:40-62 (all fields)
```go
type TranslateResult struct {
	Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
	DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
	Target         string   `json:"target"`
	Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
	Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
	Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
	Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
	Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
	Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
	Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
	Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
}
```
Note the `Warnings []string` field — engine.go:49-50 describes it as recording "non-fatal problems ... so a downgrade is never silent."

**`ChunkKind` + constants** — engine.go:87-96
```go
type ChunkKind int

const (
	// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
	ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
	// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
	ChunkDone
	// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
	ChunkError
)
```

**`Chunk`** — engine.go:99-104
```go
type Chunk struct {
	Kind   ChunkKind
	Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
	Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
	Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
}
```

**Sentinel errors (errors.go, whole file)** — style is `var (...)` block, each `errors.New("engine: <message>")` lowercase, package-prefixed, with a doc comment per var:
```go
var (
	ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
	ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
	ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
	ErrAllEnginesFailed = errors.New("engine: all engines failed")
	ErrNoDictEntry = errors.New("engine: no dictionary entry")
)
```
`ErrNoResult` ("engine: stream closed without a result") is conceptually adjacent to the truncation bug — it is returned by `Drain` (engine.go:152) when a channel closes with no terminal chunk at all.

## 2. Existing tests — NONE EXIST

There are **zero `*_test.go` files anywhere in the repository** (confirmed via `find ... -name "*_test.go"` returning nothing, and grep for `httptest`/`readSSE`/`Translate` in test files returning nothing). There is no `httptest` fake-SSE-server pattern to match. A truncation test would be the first test in `internal/engine/`. The engine is driven at runtime by the `Drain` helper (engine.go:139-153) and the TUI Update loop, and `single()` (engine.go:125-134) is the buffered-channel helper for non-streaming engines — those are the closest existing "shape" references for constructing Chunk channels in a test.

## 3. Pitfalls house style (pitfalls/README.md + go-install-module-path-mismatch.md)

**Frontmatter** is not YAML — it is bold-label lines immediately under the H1. Required fields (README.md:57-63, matched exactly by the sample file):
```markdown
# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>

**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
**First seen**: YYYY-MM
**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
```

**Section structure** (README.md:65-84) — H2 sections in this order:
- `## Symptom` — full verbatim error messages (preserves grep-ability)
- `## Root cause` — why, with source/docs/upstream link
- `## Workaround` — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
- `## Prevention` — how to avoid it again
- `## Related` — links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues

The real file `go-install-module-path-mismatch.md` follows this exactly: H1 symptom-title, the four bold labels (its `**Status**: fixed — module renamed to the GitHub path.`), then `## Symptom` / `## Root cause` / `## Workaround` / `## Prevention` / `## Related`. Slugs are kebab-case describing the symptom.

**Indexing** — YES, there is a manual index table in README.md:87-94 that a new pitfall must be added to, kept alphabetical:
```markdown
## Index

Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.

| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `duplicate-translate-on-path-...` | ... | workaround; fix in TODO P2 |
| `go-install-module-path-mismatch` | `module declares its path as: translate`, ... | fixed (module renamed) |
| `gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir` | ... | workaround (pin GOBIN) |
```
There is also a separate "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table (README.md:96-104) for traps documented elsewhere — not relevant for a net-new pitfall. The per-doc template is external, in the `project-knowledge-harness` skill (`pitfall-doc.md.template`), referenced at README.md:50-52. Related `TODO.md` links appear in pitfall `## Related` sections (e.g. the sample's line 63 references a `TODO.md` "Done" entry) — so if you add a TODO item for the fix, cross-linking it is the house convention.

## 4. stop_reason / finish_reason / message_stop / [DONE] handling

Only **one** of these four appears anywhere in `internal/engine/`:

- **`[DONE]`** — handled, OpenAI path only, llm.go:414-416:
  ```go
  if payload == "[DONE]" {
  	break
  }
  ```
  This is the OpenAI-style terminal sentinel. Note it `break`s the same as a natural loop exit — there is no flag recording that `[DONE]` was actually seen, so a stream that ends without `[DONE]` (truncation) is indistinguishable from a clean finish here.

- **`stop_reason`, `finish_reason`, `message_stop`** — **not present anywhere** in `internal/engine/` (grep returned no matches). The wire structs confirm they are never parsed:
  - `streamDelta` (llm.go:114-121) parses only `choices[].delta.content` — no `finish_reason`.
  - `anthropicStreamEvent` (llm.go:150-159) parses only `type`, `delta.{type,text}`, and `error.message` — no `message_stop`/`stop_reason` type handling. The Anthropic reader (llm.go:456) explicitly skips every event whose `Type != "content_block_delta"`, so `message_stop` / `message_delta` (which carries `stop_reason`) events are silently ignored.

**Both readers return `sc.Err()` at the end** (readSSE llm.go:432, readAnthropicSSE llm.go:466). A clean EOF makes `bufio.Scanner` return `nil` from `Err()`, so both readers return `nil` on a truncated-but-cleanly-closed stream, and the caller (llm.go:369-373 / 390) then emits a normal `ChunkDone` with whatever partial text accumulated in `full`. This is exactly the silent-truncation path described in the bug: neither the OpenAI nor the Anthropic reader records having seen its terminal marker (`[DONE]` / `message_stop`), so completeness is never asserted.

Relevant call sites for context: the streaming dispatch that turns a nil reader-return into `ChunkDone` is at llm.go:368-390 (Anthropic branch shown; the OpenAI `translate`/`readSSE` caller has the analogous structure earlier in the file).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I've reviewed the codebase and found the key structures I need to work with: the `Chunk` type with its `Kind`, `Text`, `Result`, and `Err` fields, along with the chunk token types, and `TranslateResult` already has a `Warnings` field built in. The sentinel errors follow a consistent pattern in errors.go, and there's already an `ErrNoResult` defined for when the stream closes without a result.

Since there are no existing tests in the repo, I'm weighing whether to add one. The user's request focused on fixing the core bug and documenting the pitfall, but given how hard it would be to verify SSE truncation without a live proxy, I think including a minimal httptest-based test makes sense to prove the fix works end-to-end. I'll frame it as part of the plan and let the user decide if they want to include it.

For the pitfall documentation, I'm planning to use a bold-label frontmatter format with sections like Symptoms, First seen, Affects, and Status, followed by H2 sections for deeper details.

Now for the actual fix: I need to detect truncation in both the OpenAI and Anthropic paths. For OpenAI's `readSSE`, I'll track whether `[DONE]` was seen and check `finish_reason` — if the loop exits without that marker and no error occurred, the stream was truncated. For Anthropic's `readAnthropicSSE`, I need to watch for `message_stop` events and parse the `stop_reason` from `message_delta` to catch cases like `max_tokens`. I'll need to extend the `anthropicStreamEvent` struct to capture the `stop_reason` field.

On how to surface these errors, I'm weighing two approaches: either return an error from the reader that gets translated into a `ChunkError` (which discards partial text and shows a clear error indicator), or set a truncated flag to preserve what we got. The error approach is simpler and matches what the user asked for.

But there's a wrinkle with `max_tokens` — when the model hits that limit, the stream technically completes normally with a `message_stop` event, but the content is still truncated. I need to handle that case too.

So my approach will be: if there's no terminal marker at all (missing `message_stop` or `[DONE]`), that's the connection drop bug and I'll return a truncation error. If `stop_reason` is `"max_tokens"` or `finish_reason` is `"length"`, I'll also treat that as an error since the result is genuinely incomplete. k with requiring `message_stop` — I'm not even sure copilot-proxy actually emits it on successful completion. The streaming format uses Anthropic SSE with content_block_delta events, but that doesn't guarantee a terminal marker arrives. If the proxy never sends `message_stop`, then requiring it would cause every translation to fail as truncated, which would be catastrophic. I need to verify what terminal events the proxy actually sends before I can safely commit to this error-handling strategy.

I could curl the proxy locally to inspect the raw SSE output, but that's arguably a state-changing call even though it's localhost and read-only in spirit — plan mode discourages it. Instead, I'll design the fix defensively: rather than requiring `message_stop`, I'll detect truncation through a more robust signal, though I'm aware that `message_stop` is really the only reliable completion marker in Anthropic streaming.

The core issue is whether the proxy actually forwards `message_delta` with `stop_reason` and `message_stop` events. If it strips those, there's no in-band signal at all, and I'd have to rely on HTTP-layer cues like a clean EOF versus a read error — but a clean EOF from a well-behaved proxy after a complete response looks identical to a mid-stream drop at the SSE level unless `message_stop` gets through. So the whole fix depends on verifying empirically whether the proxy forwards those events, which I'll call out in the plan and check during implementation. I'll design it defensively so the primary signal is either `message_stop` or `message_delta.stop_reason` present.

The safest approach is to first capture raw SSE output from copilot-proxy for a real completion to confirm whether terminal events come through, then implement the Anthropic reader to track `sawStop` when it sees `message_stop` and extract `stop_reason` from `message_delta`, checking on loop exit whether we got those signals. 34

160	// renderResult formats a completed translation, a dictionary entry, or a ranked
161	// "did you mean" list when a dictionary lookup missed.
162	func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
163		if res.Dictionary != nil {
164			return m.renderDictionary(res)
165		}
166		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
167			return m.renderSuggestions(res)
168		}
169		var b strings.Builder
170		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))
171	
172		if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
173			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
174		}
175		for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
176			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
177		}
178		if res.Notes != "" {
179			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
180		}
181		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
182			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.warn.Render("⚠ "+w))
183		}
184		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 {
185			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("(^e switch engine · check the model/provider)"))
186		}
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	// renderDictionary formats a dictionary lookup for the result pane.
191	func (m Model) renderDictionary(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
192		d := res.Dictionary
193		var b strings.Builder
194		head := d.Word
195		if d.Phonetic != "" {
196			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
197		}
198		b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(head))
199		for _, mn := range d.Meanings {
200			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render(mn.PartOfSpeech))
201			for i, def := range mn.Definitions {
202				if i >= 3 {
203					break
204				}
205				b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("• "+def.Text))
206			}
207		}
208		return b.String()
209	}
210	
211	// renderSuggestions renders the ranked "did you mean" list for a dictionary miss.
212	func (m Model) renderSuggestions(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
213		var b strings.Builder
214		b.WriteString(m.st.dim.Render("no exact match — did you mean:"))
215		for i, w := range res.Suggestions {
216			b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d.", i+1)) + " " + m.st.alt.Render(w))
217		}
218		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("↵ choose"))
219		return b.String()
220	}

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	}
37	
38	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
39	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
40	type TranslateResult struct {
41		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
42		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
43		Target         string   `json:"target"`
44		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
45		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
46		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
47	
48		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
49		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
50		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
51	
52		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
53		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
54		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
55	
56		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
57		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
58		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
59		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
60		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
61		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
62	}
63	
64	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
65	type DictEntry struct {
66		Word      string    `json:"word"`
67		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
68		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
69		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
70	}
71	
72	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
73	type Meaning struct {
74		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
75		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
76		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
77		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
78	}
79	
80	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
81	type Definition struct {
82		Text    string `json:"definition"`
83		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
84	}
85	
86	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
87	type ChunkKind int
88	
89	const (
90		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
91		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
92		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
93		ChunkDone
94		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
95		ChunkError
96	)
97	
98	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
99	type Chunk struct {
100		Kind   ChunkKind
101		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
102		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
103		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
104	}
105	
106	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
107	//
108	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
109	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
110	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
111	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
112	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
113	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
114	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
115	type Engine interface {
116		Name() string
117		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
118		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
119		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
120		Supports(m Mode) bool
121	}
122	
123	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
124	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
125	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
126		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
127		if err != nil {
128			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
129		} else {
130			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
131		}
132		close(ch)
133		return ch
134	}
135	
136	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
137	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
138	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
139	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
140		for c := range ch {
141			switch c.Kind {
142			case ChunkToken:
143				if onToken != nil {
144					onToken(c.Text)
145				}
146			case ChunkDone:
147				return c.Result, nil
148			case ChunkError:
149				return nil, c.Err
150			}
151		}
152		return nil, ErrNoResult
153	}
154
```
</details></tool-use>

For the HTTP timeout, I'll defer that as a follow-up task rather than core functionality. On the sentinel error question: since we're keeping partial results and not erroring on truncation, I don't need a new `ErrStreamTruncated` sentinel—the reader returns (false, nil) for max_tokens cases. The Warnings and Truncated fields on the result object carry the signal, so the CLI can check those and print warnings accordingly without requiring a separate error type.

Now I'm writing out the plan file to document this approach.
